sanskrit: अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम् । मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते ॥
hindiTranslation: जो कर्म परिणाम, हानि, हिंसा और सामर्थ्यको न देखकर मोहपूर्वक आरम्भ किया जाता है, वह तामस कहा जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: Action begun in delusion, ignoring its consequences, harm, and one’s own capacity, is called tamasic.
essence: Blind action begins where clear seeing is absent.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Action driven by confusion, without considering consequences, damage, or your real ability, is dark and unwise.
simpleInsight: You feel the warning light before rushing ahead. Clear seeing can stop avoidable damage.
detailedMeaning: On the Kurukshetra battlefield, Krishna is sorting action into three kinds for Arjuna. After describing pure and passionate action, he turns to the worst kind: action launched in confusion, without weighing results, harm, or strength.
modernRelevance: You hit send on the angry message, then realize it will wound three people and solve nothing. This verse asks for one breath of clear seeing before the damage starts.
translationLiteral: अनुबन्धम् (consequence) / क्षयम् (loss) / हिंसाम् (harm) / अनपेक्ष्य (without considering) / च (and) / पौरुषम् (one’s own capacity) / मोहात् (from delusion) / आरभ्यते (is begun) / कर्म (action) / यत् (which) / तत् (that) / तामसम् (tamasic) / उच्यते (is called)
insight: It teaches you to pause long enough to judge consequences before acting.

quoteVariants.text: Deluded action ignores harm, loss, and your own limits.
I can see the cost before I begin.
See the cost before you begin.
